Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Where can I get my hands on the Sat-Sandarbhas?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-02-06 · 4 answers
I know I can find them at ISKCON Media, but I would like to have a book since Im not much of a fan reading books online.
user [38] · 2009-02-06
The only currently available English translation (book) is Swami Tripuraris Tattva sandarbha. Its on Amazon.com etc.
user [23] · 2009-02-08
This is the one of Tattva sandarbha that I have:

http://www.krishnaculture.com/Merchant5/merchant.mvc?Screen=PROD&Store_Code=KC&Product_Code=TS&Category_Code=
user [366] · 2009-02-08
Thanks.

I found a website, rasbihari.com that I believe has all of the sandarbhas.

But theyre located in India, so Im inquiring about the cost to ship, so I know if I have to buy in bulk. I also need to ask if they are translated into English.
user [38] · 2009-02-09
I havent seen all six at Rasbihari. They are in English since Rasbihari caters mainly to Western devotees coming to Vrindavan but there is no product info. Easiest would be to have them brought by some devotee. Ask at your local temple since soon devotees will be going to Mayapur/Vrindavan.
